major_expressions: government ... shall not perish from this earth
we must live through all time, or die by suicide
cluster_profiles.persuasive_function: Makes national survival feel like the preservation of a living body rather than an abstract institutional choice.
rival_readings: Conventional body-politic idiom rather than active metaphor; keep conventionality visible in aggregate claims.
Perish can be a conventional verb for institutional failure; preserve conventionality in claims.
negative_cases: The current reviewed sample does not yet show a fully medicalized disease or purification logic.
cluster_profiles.relation_to_koenigsbergian_analysis: Supports the body-politic corollary more directly than the sacrificial law itself.

cluster_profiles.persuasive_function: Turns war loss into the possibility of renewed political life.
rival_readings: Republican renewal formula; historical-semantics controls should prevent overstating biological literalness.
negative_cases: Current evidence is concentrated in the Gettysburg climax and should not be treated as distributed across the full Lincoln corpus yet.
cluster_profiles.relation_to_koenigsbergian_analysis: Complicates preservation by adding rebirth: sacrifice does not merely keep the sacred object alive, it makes renewal imaginable.

major_expressions: The judgments of the Lord are true and righteous altogether
all the wealth ... shall be sunk
dedicated to the great task remaining before us
every drop of blood ... shall be paid by another drawn with the sword
cluster_profiles.persuasive_function: Places slavery, bloodshed, guilt, and reconciliation under divine judgment rather than partisan triumph.
political_actions_authorized: accepting war suffering as moral judgment
rival_readings: Biblical quotation may function as doctrinal citation rather than independently mapped metaphor.
Dedicated may function as conventional ceremonial diction with limited conceptual force.
Sunk may be a financial or material idiom without a full metaphorical debt structure.
Sword remains uncertain: it may be literal weapon, metonym for war, or biblical idiom.
negative_cases: Providence language can be doctrinal quotation or rhetorical convention; it should not be used as evidence of private belief without corroboration.
cluster_profiles.relation_to_koenigsbergian_analysis: Supports historical-sacral framing and guilt distribution, while limiting enemy-destruction claims through reconciliation.

major_expressions: final resting place ... gave their lives that this nation might live
gave the last full measure of devotion
cluster_profiles.persuasive_function: Converts soldier death into an offering that obligates the living.
rival_readings: Measure may be conventional quantifying language rather than a distinct accounting source domain.
The sentence may be read as solemn memorial diction rather than a fully active sacrificial mapping.
negative_cases: The current evidence sacralizes Union death but does not make killing an end in itself.
cluster_profiles.relation_to_koenigsbergian_analysis: Directly supports the sacrificial-body dimension, especially in Gettysburg's battlefield setting.
